intent.statement: Add a public documentation site that stays synchronized with the code.
success_criteria: New developer can learn Button, Card, Modal from the site alone
Site updates automatically when API changes
Site is hosted publicly and linked from README

decision_context.stakes: Low risk, high visibility. A docs site is expected by users but does not change the library API.

arbitrariness.rationale: Does not affect admissibility of construction

closure.decision: Use MDX files in /docs rendered by VitePress
closure.rationale: Markdown is familiar; MDX allows interactive examples; VitePress is simple and Vue-aligned.

closure.decision: Host on GitHub Pages via GitHub Actions
closure.rationale: Free, no new accounts, GitHub-native workflow.

closure.decision: Add CI step that validates doc examples against library build
closure.rationale: Auto-generation insufficient for narrative docs; manual review unreliable; CI catches drift at PR time.

tasks.title: Bootstrap VitePress documentation site
tasks.transformation: Add VitePress, create docs/ skeleton, add npm scripts
tasks.evidence_requirement: Local dev server runs; homepage renders
tasks.review_predicate: Reviewer can clone, install, and run docs:dev successfully
policy_obligations: Transformation potential: site must remain deployable to GitHub Pages
Semantic closure: npm script names follow conventions

tasks.title: Document the three main widgets
tasks.transformation: Write MDX pages for Button, Card, Modal with examples
tasks.review_predicate: New developer can use widgets without reading source
policy_obligations: Functional properties: docs must not break existing CI
Verification evidence: examples must be validated

tasks.title: Add CI validation for doc examples
tasks.evidence_requirement: Invalid example in test PR fails CI
tasks.review_predicate: Invalid examples caught; valid pass; < 2 min runtime
policy_obligations: Functional properties: doc examples execute without error
Verification evidence: invalid examples fail CI

tasks.title: Deploy to GitHub Pages
tasks.transformation: GitHub Actions deploys docs on push to main
policy_obligations: Transformation potential: must remain deployable to GitHub Pages
Compatibility: must not require vendor migration

review_predicates.statement: Reviewer can clone, install, and run docs:dev successfully

admissibility_policies.name: Required CIS Policy
admissibility_policies.description: A required constructive invariance policy that governs all construction states for this system.

functional_properties: All public API contracts remain backward-compatible for one major version
Documentation examples must execute without error against the current library build
transformation_potential: The system must remain deployable to GitHub Pages without vendor migration
New widget documentation pages can be added without changing site generator
compatibility_or_migration: Schema changes require a migration script and a rollback path
Removed features must be documented with removal rationale and replacement path
verification_evidence: CI must run integration tests before merge
Doc examples must be validated against the built library in CI
Review must check that no public API contract was broken
semantic_closure: The term 'widget' refers only to UI components in the public API
Version numbers follow semver and are not reused

admissibility_policies.residual_on_failure: Policy failure becomes a failed_review or missing_policy residual. Work cannot integrate until the residual is resolved or the policy is explicitly relaxed by principal authority.

residuals.description: Sidebar structure may need grouping when T2 adds widget pages
Dark mode toggle not explicitly configured
